veteran.reason: This bill addresses nonconsensual intimate visual depictions and applies to the general public regardless of veteran status; it has no distinct effect on veterans.

has_disability.reason: The bill addresses nonconsensual intimate visual depictions and applies identically regardless of disability status.

student.reason: This bill addresses nonconsensual intimate visual depictions and platform takedown obligations; it applies to individuals regardless of student status, with no trait-specific effect on enrolled students.

has_student_loans.reason: This bill concerns nonconsensual intimate visual depictions and platform takedown obligations; it has no bearing on student loan status.

parent_of_minor.reason: The bill contains specific provisions criminalizing nonconsensual intimate depictions and digital forgeries of minors and establishes a takedown process that an authorized person may use on a minor's behalf. Parents of minor children are first-order affected because they would typically act to invoke these protections and removal requests for their children.

caregiver_of_adult.reason: The removal and notification process allows an authorized person to act on behalf of an identifiable individual, so caregivers may invoke it for a dependent adult who cannot self-advocate. This is a first-order indirect relevance, though the bill's adult protections apply the same regardless of caregiver status.

homeowner.reason: The bill concerns nonconsensual intimate imagery and platform takedown obligations, unrelated to housing status.

renter.reason: No connection to renting or housing arrangements.

citizen.reason: The bill protects individuals from nonconsensual intimate depictions regardless of citizenship status; the effect is identical across all statuses.

permanent_resident.reason: The protections and prohibitions apply the same way regardless of immigration status.

other_status.reason: The bill's effect does not vary based on immigration status.

single.reason: The bill addresses nonconsensual intimate imagery and applies regardless of marital status.

married.reason: Effect is identical across marital statuses; marriage is irrelevant.

divorced.reason: No divorce-specific provisions; effect does not vary by marital status.

widowed.reason: No widowhood-related provisions; effect is identical regardless of marital status.

under_18.reason: The bill contains distinct provisions for minors, defining 'minor' as under 18 and imposing different intent standards and enhanced criminal penalties (3 years vs. 2 for adult offenses; 30 vs. 18 months for threats) when depictions involve minors, making minors an explicit subject with trait-specific treatment.

age_18_25.reason: Adults are covered as 'not a minor,' and the bill treats all adult age groups identically.

age_26_49.reason: Adults are covered as 'not a minor,' and the bill treats all adult age groups identically.

age_50_64.reason: Adults are covered as 'not a minor,' and the bill treats all adult age groups identically.

age_65_plus.reason: Adults are covered as 'not a minor,' and the bill treats all adult age groups identically.

under_30k.reason: The bill criminalizes nonconsensual intimate imagery and mandates platform takedowns, applying to all individuals regardless of income.

income_30k_60k.reason: Protections and prohibitions apply uniformly across income levels.

income_60k_120k.reason: No income-dependent effect; the law applies equally regardless of household income.

income_120k_250k.reason: The bill's effect does not vary by income.

income_250k_plus.reason: No differential effect based on income.

education.reason: The bill does not touch teaching or school work.

healthcare.reason: A narrow medical-purpose disclosure exception exists, but the bill does not meaningfully govern clinical work.

manufacturing.reason: No relevance to industrial production work.

agriculture.reason: No relevance to farming or related work.

transportation_logistics.reason: No relevance to transport or logistics work.

public_sector_first_responders.reason: Law enforcement activities are carved out as exceptions, but the bill does not impose duties or effects on their occupation itself.

professional_office.reason: Only incidental exceptions for legal filings; no direct effect on office work.

retail_service.reason: No relevance to retail or service work.

technology.reason: Covered online platforms and interactive computer services must build notice-and-removal systems, meet a 48-hour takedown deadline, and face FTC enforcement, directly imposing obligations on software, IT, and platform workers.

other.reason: No distinct occupational group beyond those listed is directly targeted.

employee.reason: The bill targets criminal disclosure of intimate imagery and platform takedown duties; its effects do not vary by employment status.

self_employed_owner.reason: Obligations fall on covered platforms as entities, not on self-employed status generally, so the trait is not determinative.

unemployed.reason: Irrelevant to work status; the bill concerns intimate visual depictions and platform obligations.

retired.reason: No connection to retirement or work status.

employer_plan.reason: This bill concerns nonconsensual intimate imagery and platform takedown obligations; health coverage is irrelevant.

medicare.reason: No connection to health insurance of any kind.

medicaid.reason: No connection to health insurance of any kind.

marketplace_individual.reason: No connection to health insurance of any kind.

va_tricare.reason: No connection to health insurance of any kind.

uninsured.reason: No connection to health insurance of any kind.

male.reason: The protections apply identically to any identifiable individual regardless of gender.

female.reason: Although victims of nonconsensual intimate imagery skew female, the bill's legal effect is identical across genders, so overrepresentation alone does not raise the score.

nonbinary_other.reason: The bill's provisions apply the same regardless of gender identity.

lgbtq.reason: This bill addresses nonconsensual intimate visual depictions and deepfakes applying to all individuals regardless of sexual orientation or gender identity, with no differential effect on LGBTQ+ people.

christian.reason: The bill addresses nonconsensual intimate imagery online and applies regardless of religion.

other_criteria: individuals depicted in intimate visual depictions (potential victims)
persons who publish such depictions (potential offenders)
target_groups.reason: The bill criminalizes nonconsensual publication of intimate depictions of any identifiable individual and creates removal rights, applying broadly to the general public as both potential victims and offenders.

other_criteria: depicted as an identifiable minor in an intimate visual depiction or digital forgery
target_groups.reason: The bill contains distinct offense standards and heightened penalties for intimate depictions involving minors.

other_criteria: operators of covered online platforms hosting user-generated content
target_groups.reason: Covered platforms must establish notice-and-removal processes and face FTC enforcement for noncompliance.
